steps.title: Spot the noun clause
steps.content: The blank is in a noun clause introduced by “how,” with the subject “his barbershop.”

steps.title: Apply the verb form rule
steps.content: A noun clause requires a finite verb as its predicate; “served” is the only finite form and correctly marks the past tense. The remaining choices are all non-finite and cannot function as the sole verb in a noun clause. That matches Choice C — served.
